root_motivation: Turn hand-discovered, re-found-each-tick anomalies about inert wintermute kernel primitives (memlog/agentns/warden/provfs) into a structured, one-command read-only liveness detector — the sibling of binstale/vigil but for the never-activated axis rather than the stale-but-running axis.
user_persona: Joe (wintermute admin) running `quicken probe` after any kernel or package update to confirm every primitive is Live before trusting the system; also used as a cron-gate in the build pipeline.

unfakeable_metric.name: acceptance_tests_passing
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | awk '{print $4}' | paste -sd+ | bc

acceptance_criteria.description: `cargo build --release` produces a `quicken` binary; `quicken probe --help` lists the probe subcommand and the `--json` flag.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac1.rs — run `quicken probe --help` and assert `--json` appears in output; assert binary exists at target/release/quicken

acceptance_criteria.description: Each of the four probes returns the correct Verdict against a fixture ProbeEnv with known inputs (golden tests): fixture /dev/memlog with real-world perms + no group membership → InstalledNotActivated; all-zeros agent_session → Inert; comm:-form provfs xattr → LiveDegraded; 128-bit-hex → Live.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs — fixture-based golden tests for all four probes

acceptance_criteria.description: MemlogProbe returns StagedNotInstalled when the fixture pkg dir contains a higher pkgrel than the fixture installed version, and the evidence records both pkgrels (deterministic on the known 5-vs-11 fixture).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs — pkgrel fixture with installed=5, staged=11

acceptance_criteria.description: `quicken probe --json` emits valid JSON deserializable back into Vec<PrimitiveReport> (round-trip test).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs — run binary with --json, deserialize output

acceptance_criteria.description: `quicken probe` exits non-zero when any primitive verdict is worse than LiveDegraded, and zero when all are Live/LiveDegraded (tested via two fixture environments).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac5.rs — integration test via two ProbeEnv fixtures

acceptance_criteria.description: No probe panics on a missing surface (absent dev node, unreadable proc file, no xattr) — each maps to Unknown or Inert with evidence, never a crash.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac6.rs — all probes against completely empty tmpdir, assert no panic and verdict != panic

acceptance_criteria.description: Tests perform zero network access and zero writes outside the test tmpdir; a test asserts the probe path is pure-read (cloud-build-safe).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac7.rs — run test suite under network-blocked env and assert exit 0

scope: Workspace at ~/wintermute/quicken with quicken-probe lib crate and quicken binary
Four probes: MemlogProbe, AgentnsProbe, WardenProbe, ProvfsProbe
Verdict enum: Live, LiveDegraded, StagedNotInstalled, InstalledNotActivated, Inert, Unknown
Evidence struct with key/value pairs and free-text detail
CLI: quicken probe and quicken probe --json
Non-zero exit when any primitive worse than LiveDegraded
non_goals: Not a daemon — quicken-probe is a read-only CLI, not a monitoring daemon
Not a fixer — it only classifies, never writes or attempts to activate primitives
Not a replacement for vigil/binstale — different axis (never-activated vs stale-but-running)
No network calls — fully offline, no remote lookup of package info
No write operations — read-only throughout (no /proc writes, no pkg installs)

additional.sigpipe_reset: first line of main() per self_sigpipe_panic_toolkit

five_whys_trace.q: Why do you want a CLI that probes kernel primitive liveness?
five_whys_trace.a: Because today I discover inert primitives by running three separate bash commands and reading the journal — each tick re-discovers the same anomaly.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is re-discovering the same anomaly each tick a problem?
five_whys_trace.a: Because it costs time and the drift widens unnoticed — installed pkgrel is 5, built pkgrel is 11, and nothing structurally tracks this gap.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the gap widening go unnoticed?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the signal is only in the journal as free-text — no structured detector exists for the never-activated axis.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does a structured detector solve this when journaling didn't?
five_whys_trace.a: Because a read-only CLI can be a pipeline gate: CI blocks on inert primitives, self-review calls it, and the verdict is machine-parseable JSON.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should quicken-probe be a separate crate or merged with quicken binary?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Separate lib crate (quicken-probe) + thin binary (quicken) in a workspace, so sibling PRDs (quicken-attest, quicken-crossdep, quicken-remedy) can depend on the lib without re-importing the CLI.
